{
  "name": "watch Skill review",
  "overview": "Review of [SKILL.md](SKILL.md) for the &#96;watch&#96; video skill: strengths, concrete usability fixes (including one doc/code mismatch), and architectural options to reduce duplication and fragile agent steps.",
  "plan": "# Review: &#96;watch&#96; Skill — usability and architecture\n\n## What works well\n\n- **Clear operational contract:** numbered Steps 0–5, explicit “silent on success” for setup, mandatory &#96;summary.md&#96; persistence (Step 4b), and a solid **Security \u0026 Permissions** section — this is unusually thorough for agent-facing skills.\n- **Runtime split matches the doc:** [scripts/watch.py](scripts/watch.py) delegates to &#96;download&#96;, &#96;frames&#96;, &#96;transcribe&#96;, &#96;whisper&#96; — sensible separation for maintenance and testing ([test/](test/) exists alongside [scripts/setup.py](scripts/setup.py)).\n- **Dual distribution is thought through:** [.gitattributes](.gitattributes) + [scripts/build-skill.sh](scripts/build-skill.sh) keep Claude Code’s archive (hooks/commands/plugin) while stripping to a lean claude.ai &#96;.skill&#96; bundle under the 200-file cap.\n\n## Usability improvements (SKILL + small docs)\n\n1. **Add a “Quick path” block at the top (after frontmatter)** — 8–12 lines: default invocation pattern (&#96;setup.py --check&#96; → &#96;watch.py&#96; → parallel &#96;Read&#96; frames → answer → &#96;save_summary.py&#96;), plus the one-line rule “exact chat reply → &#96;summary.md&#96;”. Agents often benefit from a scannable anchor before the long tables.\n\n2. **Fix the misleading &#96;--fps&#96; example** — The SKILL says fps is “clamped to 2 fps max” (accurate: [scripts/frames.py](scripts/frames.py) &#96;MAX_FPS = 2.0&#96;, enforced in [scripts/watch.py](scripts/watch.py) &#96;fps = min(args.fps, MAX_FPS)&#96;), but the example uses &#96;--fps 3&#96;. Replace with &#96;--fps 2&#96; or remove &#96;--fps&#96; and describe density via &#96;--start&#96;/&#96;--end&#96; + auto mode so examples match behavior.\n\n3. **Surface environment variable differences early** — The SKILL consistently uses &#96;${CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR}&#96;; the SessionStart hook uses &#96;${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}&#96; in [hooks/hooks.json](hooks/hooks.json) / [hooks/scripts/check-setup.sh](hooks/scripts/check-setup.sh). Add a short note: “On Claude Code plugin installs, paths may be exposed as &#96;CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT&#96;; on claude.ai skills, use &#96;CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR&#96; as documented.” Reduces agent confusion when the same markdown is read in two runtimes.\n\n4. **Windows callout** — Step 0 already says &#96;python3&#96; → &#96;python&#96; on Windows; consider a single **Platform** subsection (Windows / macOS / Linux) so the main flow stays linear.\n\n5. **Step 4b friction** — Heredoc + absolute &#96;WORK&#96; path is correct but easy for models to get wrong (wrong path, unquoted delimiter, truncation). Usability options (pick one or combine lightly):\n   - Document a **minimal checklist**: “copy &#96;_Work dir:&#96; path verbatim”, “use &#96;\u003c\u003c'EOF'&#96;”, “paste full reply unchanged”.\n   - **Optional architecture change (see below):** have &#96;watch.py&#96; print a **single-line machine marker** (e.g. &#96;WATCH_WORK_DIR=/abs/path&#96;) in addition to markdown, or support &#96;--print-work-dir-only&#96; for a trivial parse step.\n\n6. **Revisit “skip Step 0 on follow-up”** — Reasonable for speed, but if the user fixes deps mid-session, the model might skip a now-stale success. Softer wording: “May skip Step 0 after a successful &#96;--check&#96; in the same session unless the user changed their environment or a later step fails with missing binary.”\n\n## Architecture improvements\n\n&#96;&#96;&#96;mermaid\nflowchart LR\n  subgraph today [Current flow]\n    Hook[SessionStart hook bash]\n    Step0[SKILL Step 0 setup.py --check]\n    Watch[watch.py]\n    Hook --\u003e Step0\n    Step0 --\u003e Watch\n  end\n&#96;&#96;&#96;\n\n- **Duplication between hook and Python preflight** — The hook re-implements “ffmpeg + yt-dlp + keys + SETUP_COMPLETE” in bash; Step 0 is the canonical &#96;setup.py --check&#96; / &#96;--json&#96;. **Options:** (a) document that the hook is UX-only and Step 0 remains authoritative; (b) thin the hook to &#96;python3 .../setup.py --check&#96; (or &#96;--json&#96;) with a tiny wrapper for stderr messaging — one source of truth, less drift.\n\n- **Stable agent contract from &#96;watch.py&#96;** — Today the model must parse markdown and a footer line for &#96;_Work dir:&#96;. A **structured tail** (JSON on stderr or a final stdout line after &#96;---&#96;) would improve reliability for summary path, frame list, and transcript path without removing the human-readable report.\n\n- **Summary persistence** — Architecturally cleaner long-term: &#96;watch.py&#96; could write &#96;summary.md&#96; if stdin or &#96;--summary-file&#96; is supplied after the model’s answer, or emit a small helper that takes **file path to the reply** instead of heredoc. That reduces shell-quoting hazards; tradeoff is more CLI surface area.\n\n- **Command vs skill** — [commands/watch.md](commands/watch.md) is already a thin delegator; keep it that way and ensure any “quick path” lives in SKILL only (or one line in the command pointing to Step 1) to avoid double maintenance.\n\n## Non-goals / low priority\n\n- Rewriting the whole SKILL into multiple files unless the host supports includes (usually not) — internal **anchor headings** and a quick-reference block give most of the benefit.\n- Changing the 200-file packaging strategy unless the archive grows — current [build-skill.sh](scripts/build-skill.sh) approach is documented and appropriate.\n\n## Suggested implementation order (when you exit plan mode)\n\n1. Fix the &#96;--fps 3&#96; example and any similar inconsistencies in SKILL.\n2. Add quick-path + &#96;CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR&#96; / &#96;CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT&#96; note + soften Step 0 skip language.\n3. Optionally: refactor hook to delegate to &#96;setup.py&#96;; optionally add machine-readable work dir from &#96;watch.py&#96;.\n",

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is what was going on and what we added to the plan.

**Why you saw `watch-_546e_9f`:** The default work directory comes from `tempfile.mkdtemp(prefix="watch-", dir=~/yt-videos)` in [`scripts/watch.py`](scripts/watch.py) (`default_watch_work_dir`). That always appends a random suffix so two runs never collide; it is not tied to the YouTube (or other) video id.

**What you asked for:** Prefer a stable name such as `watch-<videoId>` (for YouTube, the id from the URL or from yt-dlp’s metadata for other sites).

The plan file is updated with a dedicated section **“Default work directory: use video id”** and a new todo **`work-dir-video-id`**. The approach documented there is:

- **YouTube:** derive id from the URL before download (same logic as `transcript_filename_for_source`), sanitize, use `~/yt-videos/watch-<id>`, with a numeric suffix if that folder already exists.
- **Other URLs:** after download, read `id` from `video.info.json` and rename the work directory to `watch-<id>` when safe.
- **Local files:** either `watch-<sanitized filename stem>` or keep random names and document the choice.

_**Agent (default)**_

Here is what was implemented from the plan.

### Work directory naming ([`scripts/watch.py`](scripts/watch.py))
- **YouTube** (watch URL, Shorts, embed, `youtu.be`): default dir is `~/yt-videos/watch-<videoId>` using the same id rules as transcripts; collisions get `-2`, `-3`, …
- **Local files**: `~/yt-videos/watch-<sanitizedStem>` with the same collision pattern.
- **Other URLs**: still start as a `tempfile.mkdtemp` `watch-…` folder, then after yt-dlp writes `video.info.json`, **`maybe_promote_work_dir`** renames to `watch-<yt-dlp id>` when it does not already match.
- **`refresh_download_paths`** in [`scripts/download.py`](scripts/download.py) re-resolves `video_path` / `subtitle_path` after a rename so paths stay valid.
- **`default_watch_work_dir()`** remains for compatibility (random `mkdtemp` under `~/yt-videos`).
- **stderr** line: `WATCH_WORK_DIR=<absolute path>` at the end of the run (with the markdown footer unchanged).

### YouTube URL parsing
- Shorts and `/embed/` ids are included in `_youtube_video_id`, so folder + `yt-*.md` names stay aligned for those URL shapes.

### Docs
- **[`SKILL.md`](SKILL.md)**: Quick path, `CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT` vs `CLAUDE_SKILL_DIR`, softer Step 0 skip wording, corrected `--fps` example (max 2), updated `--out-dir` / security copy, Step 4b checklist + `WATCH_WORK_DIR`.
- **[`README.md`](README.md)** and **[`commands/watch.md`](commands/watch.md)**: default dir wording aligned.
- **[`test/README.md`](test/README.md)**: describes the new behavior.

### Tests ([`test/test_watch_transcript_output.py`](test/test_watch_transcript_output.py))
- New cases for YouTube watch/Shorts dirs, collision suffix, Vimeo → `mkdtemp`, local stem, Shorts transcript filename.

### Not done (optional from the plan)
- **SessionStart hook → `setup.py --check`**: not changed, because `setup.py --check` treats “no API key” as failure while the hook stays silent when binaries exist and captions-only is OK. Changing that would regress plugin startup UX.

Run tests from repo root:

```bash
python3 -m unittest discover -s test
```

_**User**_

Where is the default dir to save to?

---

_**Agent (default)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The default directory for saving watch output is typically the current working directory unless specified otherwise.</details></think>

_**Agent (default)**_

Default artifacts go under your **home directory**, in **`~/yt-videos/`** (i.e. `Path.home() / "yt-videos"`).

Each run uses a subdirectory there, for example `~/yt-videos/watch-<videoId>` for YouTube, unless you pass **`--out-dir`** to pick another path.

Defined here:

```60:64:scripts/watch.py
def _yt_videos_root() -> Path:
    root = Path.home() / "yt-videos"
    root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
    return root
```

`initial_auto_work_dir()` builds the concrete `watch-…` folder name under that root (see the same file).

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user reports that files were not saved to the ~/yt-videos directory. When a skill runs in Claude's hosted environment, `Path.home()` may resolve to a sandbox home like `/home/claude` rather than the user's local Mac home. This means files saved to `~/yt-videos` might land in the execution environments home instead of the expected local directory. The default output path is `Path.home() / "yt-videos"`, where `~` refers to the process's `HOME` environment variable. If `--out-dir` is specified, it overrides this default. The `md` file is only written by `save_summary.py` after the answer is generated, not by `watch.py`. To improve clarity, the tool should print the absolute output path at startup, similar to how it shows the working directory. This would help users confirm they're saving to the intended location. A diagnostic message showing the resolved `HOME` or full root path could also reduce confusion. [watch] artifact root: /home/claude/yt-videos (HOME=/home/claude)

To improve clarity, the tool will now print the resolved home directory alongside the working directory at startup. This change helps users verify the actual save location and understand whether files are being written to the local machine or a sandbox environment.</details></think>
